questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I used to live in Paris, ___"
questions.answer: but now I live in Madrid.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She didn't use to like coffee, ___"
questions.answer: but now she drinks three cups a day.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "He used to drive to work, ___"
questions.answer: but now he cycles everywhere.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "They used to be close friends, ___"
questions.answer: but they don't talk anymore.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "We didn't use to have a TV, ___"
questions.answer: but now we watch it every evening.

questions.prompt: I _____ (used to / live) in a small town, but now I live in a big city.
questions.answer: used to live

questions.prompt: She _____ (didn't use to / like) spicy food as a child.
questions.answer: didn't use to like

questions.prompt: They _____ (used to / play) football every Sunday.
questions.answer: used to play

questions.prompt: He _____ (didn't use to / have) a beard, but now he does.
questions.answer: didn't use to have

questions.prompt: We _____ (used to / walk) to school when we were young.
questions.answer: used to walk

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I use to play tennis every weekend."
questions.answer: In positive sentences, always use 'used to' (with -d): 'I used to play'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She didn't used to eat meat."
questions.answer: After 'didn't', there is no -d on 'use': 'She didn't use to eat meat'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "They used to lived near the beach."
questions.answer: After 'used to', use the base infinitive: 'They used to live'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "He used to has long hair when he was a student."
questions.answer: After 'used to', use the base form: 'He used to have long hair'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Did she used to work here?"
questions.answer: In questions with 'did', use 'use to' (no -d): 'Did she use to work here?'
I used to cycle to school.
She didn't use to drink coffee, but now she does.
Did you use to live here as a child?
They used to play chess every Sunday.
He didn't use to eat vegetables, but now he loves them.
